title: Avro
documentation:
## Purpose
Apache Avro is a row-oriented binary serialization format that embeds its schema as JSON in the file header. The [Avro](https://capdag.com/browse/media:avro;list;record) media type represents files that store an ordered list of records encoded under a shared Avro schema.

## When to use
Use this type when schema evolution is a priority — Avro's reader/writer schema resolution allows producers and consumers to evolve their schemas independently. It is commonly used in event streaming pipelines (Kafka), Hadoop-based data lakes, and RPC frameworks that need compact, self-describing records.

## Notes
The embedded schema allows readers to decode the file without any out-of-band schema registry, though many production deployments use a separate registry for schema management. Implementers should use the official Apache Avro library for the target language; schema fingerprinting via the `avro.schema.Schema` fingerprint methods helps detect incompatible changes.
